```{r}
full_CP_per.var <- 100 * (1 - full_CP_oob.mse/ var(CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR, na.rm = TRUE))
print(full_CP_per.var)

range_CHLOR <- max(exp(CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR)) - min(exp(CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR))

#In-bag RMSE
full_CP_rmse<- rmse(exp(full_CP$predicted), exp(CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR))
full_CP_rmse.scaled <- full_CP_rmse/range_CHLOR
print(paste0('In-bag RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_rmse,2), " (mg/L)"))
print(paste0('Scaled In-bag RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_rmse.scaled*100,2), "%"))

#OOB RMSE
full_CP_oob.rmse<- rmse(exp(full_CP$predicted.oob), exp(CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR))
full_CP_oob.rmse.scaled <- full_CP_oob.rmse/range_CHLOR
print(paste0('OOB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_oob.rmse,2), " (mg/L)"))
print(paste0('Scaled OOB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_oob.rmse.scaled*100,2), "%"))

# Model tuning - How many trees? (default: 500)
#full_CP$err.rate

# VIP
plot(full_CP, plots.one.page=FALSE)
full_CP_vimp <- sort(full_CP$importance, decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]
par(mar=c(5,8.5,4,1)+.1)
barplot(full_CP_vimp, horiz = TRUE, cex.names = 0.5, las = 1, main = "CHLOR full_CP model VIMP")

#print(vimp(full_CP)$importance)

# Make a df for plots
full_CP_pred.oob <- data.frame(obs = CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR, pred = full_CP$predicted.oob, predictions = "OOB") # The prediction is computed using only the trees in which that specific case was not used for training.
full_CP_pred.ib <- data.frame(obs =CHLOR_CP$logCHLOR, pred = full_CP$predicted, predictions = "In-Bag") # This contains the predicted values for each case in the training data based on the entire ensemble (all trees). It uses the data that was "in-bag" (used to grow the trees) to determine the terminal node predictions.
full_CP_plotdf <- rbind(full_CP_pred.oob, full_CP_pred.ib) %>% 
  mutate(obs = exp(obs)) %>% 
  mutate(pred = exp(pred)) %>% 
  mutate(delta = obs - pred)

# Plot
ggplot(data = full_CP_plotdf, aes(x = pred, y = obs, color = predictions, shape = predictions)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(title = "CHLOR full_CP Model",
       x = "Predicted values (mg/L)",
       y = "Observed values (mg/L)") +
  theme_bw() + 
  geom_abline() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = 80, label = paste0('OOB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_oob.rmse,2), ' | scaled RMSE = ', round(full_CP_oob.rmse.scaled*100,2), "%"), size = 3) +
    annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = 100, label = paste0('IB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_rmse,2), ' | scaled RMSE = ', round(full_CP_rmse.scaled*100,2), "%"), size = 3) +
      annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = 125, label = paste0('% variance explained = ' , round(full_CP_per.var,2), '%'), size = 3) 

# CREATE DF FOR MAPPING PURPOSES
# Need to join predictions to AWQMS columns, but there are no unique identifiers in the CHLOR table. Predictions will be in the same order as the CHLOR table initially.

# Find a StreamCat row that has ALL unique values
# any(duplicated(CHLOR_CP$Coast_dist))  # Coast dist works.
# any(duplicated(CHLOR$PRECIP9120WS))

# Join predictions to StreamCat table
full_CP_obs.predict <- cbind(full_CP_plotdf, CHLOR_CP) %>% 
  filter(predictions == "OOB") %>% 
  arrange(Coast_dist) # Order by unique col

full_CP_awqms <- chlor_vars_selected %>% 
  arrange(Coast_dist) %>% # Order by unique col
  select(MLocID:logCHLOR) 

# Join obs.pred with awqms table, and makes sure the CHLOR results match (Make sure logCHLOR column is the same as logCHLOR.1)
full_CP_map <- cbind(full_CP_obs.predict,full_CP_awqms)

# Then select to remove duplicate logCHLOR column and reorder.
full_CP_map <- full_CP_map %>% 
  select(obs:COMID) %>% 
  relocate(MLocID:COMID, .before = obs)

pal <- colorNumeric(palette = "Greens", 
  domain = c(0, full_CP_map$obs))
pal2 <- colorNumeric(palette = "Greens", 
  domain = c(0, full_CP_map$obs))
pal3 <- colorNumeric(palette = "RdBu", domain = c(-1 * max(full_CP_map$delta),max(full_CP_map$delta)))

map_function(full_CP_map)

CHLOR_CP_no.hi <- CHLOR_CP %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(c("CANALDENSWS", "DAMDENSWS", "FERTWS", "NPDESDENSWS", "PCTAGDRAINAGEWS", "PCTAGSLPMID2001WS", "PCTCROP2001WS", "PCTIMP2001WS",  "PCTURBHI2001WS", "PCTURBLO2001WS","PCTURBOP2001WS", "PESTIC1997WS", "POPDEN2010WS", "RDCRSSLPWTDWS", "RDCRSWS", "RDDENSWS", "SEPTICWS",  "WWTPMAJORDENSWS", "WWTPALLDENSWS")), ~0))

# Make predictions with adjusted human-influence to 0 
full_CP_no.hi <- predict(full_CP, newdata= CHLOR_CP_no.hi, seed = 24)

# Mean squared error (untransformed)
full_CP_mse_no.hi <- tail(full_CP_no.hi$err.rate,1)
full_CP_per.var_no.hi <- 100 * (1 - full_CP_mse_no.hi/ var(CHLOR_CP_no.hi$logCHLOR, na.rm = TRUE))

# RMSE
full_CP_rmse_no.hi<- rmse(exp(full_CP_no.hi$predicted), exp(CHLOR_CP_no.hi$logCHLOR))
full_CP_rmse.scaled_no.hi <- full_CP_rmse_no.hi/range_CHLOR
print(paste0('OOB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_rmse_no.hi,2), " (mg/L)"))
print(paste0('Scaled OOB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_rmse.scaled_no.hi*100,2), "%"))

# Make a df for plots
full_CP_plotdf_no.hi <- data.frame(obs = CHLOR_CP_no.hi$logCHLOR, pred = full_CP_no.hi$predicted) %>% 
  mutate(obs = exp(obs)) %>% 
  mutate(pred = exp(pred)) %>% 
  mutate(delta = obs - pred)

# Plot
ggplot(data = full_CP_plotdf_no.hi, aes(x = pred, y = obs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(title = "CHLOR_CP full_CP Model predictions without human influenced vars",
       x = "Predicted values (mg/L)",
       y = "Observed values (mg/L)") +
  theme_bw() + 
  geom_abline() + 
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  annotate("text", x =0.9, y = 100, label = paste0('OOB RMSE = ' , round(full_CP_rmse_no.hi,2), ' | scaled RMSE = ', round(full_CP_rmse.scaled_no.hi*100,2), "%"), size = 3)

# Plot residuals - full_CP model, adjusted human-influenced vars to 0
full_CP_resid_no.hi <- exp(CHLOR_CP_no.hi$logCHLOR) - exp(full_CP_no.hi$predicted) # Observed minus predicted oob
plot(full_CP_resid_no.hi ~ exp(full_CP_no.hi$predicted), xlab = "Predicted CHLOR_CP (mg/L)", ylab = "Residuals (obs - pred)", main = "full_CP model residuals, no HI vars")
abline(h=0, col = "red", lty = 2)

```
